name: Explore Codebase
desc: Investigate an unfamiliar area
prompt: /goat-debug investigate this codebase - ask me which area to focus on, or start with a high-level map

name: Diagnose Error
desc: Diagnose mode: hypotheses → root cause, stop before fix
prompt: /goat-debug diagnose an error - ask me to paste the error message, stack trace, and what I was doing when it happened

name: Fix Bug
desc: Diagnose mode, full path through to post-fix verification
prompt: /goat-debug fix a bug - ask me for expected vs actual behaviour and steps to reproduce, then run through to post-fix verification

name: Code Review
desc: Two-pass review of recent changes
prompt: /goat-review my recent changes

name: Quality Audit
desc: Area audit of the most-changed files
prompt: /goat-review audit the most-changed files in this repo

name: Review Uncommitted
desc: Pre-commit gate on uncommitted changes
prompt: /goat-review my uncommitted changes as a pre-commit gate - flag MUST findings only, skip MAY nitpicks

name: Review Instructions
desc: Audit router files for staleness
prompt: /goat-review audit AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, and .github/copilot-instructions.md for staleness, contradictions, missing verification gates, and broken cross-references

name: Critique GOAT Flow
desc: Area audit of the goat-flow installation
prompt: /goat-review audit the goat-flow setup in this project - read-only scope covers AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, .goat-flow/config.yaml, .goat-flow/skill-reference/, installed skills under .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .github/skills/, plus hooks and active-agent config surfaces. Report setup quality, skill usability, contradictions, and stale docs with evidence.

name: Plan Feature
desc: Feature brief → plan
prompt: /goat-plan a new feature - ask me for the feature brief (problem, users, success criteria)

name: Break Into Milestones
desc: Milestone task files with exit criteria
prompt: /goat-plan break this into milestones - ask me which feature or plan if not already in context

name: Plan Refactor
desc: Plan a refactor with blast-radius analysis
prompt: /goat-plan a refactor - ask me what to restructure and the expected blast radius (callers, tests, dependencies, config)

name: Critique a Plan
desc: Multi-lens critique of a plan
prompt: /goat-critique this plan

name: Critique an Artifact
desc: Multi-lens critique of an assessment, strategy, or review findings
prompt: /goat-critique an artifact - ask me what to critique (assessment, strategy, review findings, test strategy, architecture proposal, etc.) if not already in context

name: Pressure-Test a Skill
desc: RED/GREEN/REFACTOR skill TDD
prompt: /goat-critique pressure-test a goat-flow skill using .goat-flow/skill-reference/skill-quality-testing.md - ask me which skill, dispatch a fresh sub-agent with a realistic 3+ pressure scenario (target skill NOT pre-loaded), capture RED rationalizations verbatim, propose GREEN counters only for classes that reproduced, iterate RED → GREEN → REFACTOR to bulletproof (3 clean passes) or cap at 5 iterations with Decision Debt in .goat-flow/decisions/. Propagate edits to all skill dirs, then re-run goat-flow audit --check-drift.

name: Testing Gap & Overlap Analysis
desc: PR-scoped gap & overlap analysis with time-budgeted plan
prompt:
/goat-qa Analyse the testing gaps in my current PR (gh pr view on the current
branch; if no PR, use commits between HEAD and deploy).

Scope the plan to what I will ACTUALLY run today:
- modality: [UI | SQL | CLI | mixed - ask if unclear]
- time budget: [N minutes]
- environment: [local | mirror | production-verify-only]
- ticket type: [bug | feature | devops]

Exclude what's already covered. For each of these, tell me in ONE LINE
what it proves for THIS PR (so I can sanity-check your assumptions):
- PHPStan (type/null safety, undefined methods/constants)
- Symfony + Doctrine validation in CI
- Access-control scanner in CI (note: known false-negative rate -
  ~150 flagged routes; not a substitute for per-endpoint checks)
- Existing PHPUnit tests touching changed symbols (grep tests/). Note:
  unit test sparsity is by design in this codebase, not a gap.
- Existing Cypress tests touching changed flows - NAME the test files
  and assess assertion depth (page-loads-only vs verifies controls/data).
  Do not assume shallow.
- Dev's own testing notes on the PR
- Copilot reviewer comments on the PR (surface, don't duplicate)

Then, before proposing tests, answer these four questions:

A. ENTRY POINTS: list every path that triggers the changed code
   (portal, API, OLB, cron, webhook). Flag any where behaviour may differ.
B. PRODUCTION DATA SHAPE: does this touch columns that could be NULL,
   legacy, or from abandoned workflows in production but clean in base
   data? Name the columns.
C. ACCESS CONTROL: does this touch patient/practitioner data or cross
   PracGroup/LinkPaG boundaries? If yes, list endpoints that need IDOR
   and tenant-boundary probing.
D. PRIOR INCIDENTS: search the repo for the changed files/symbols -
   has this area broken before? Link the tickets.

Label every claim in A–D as VERIFIED (read from code/config) or
INFERRED (pattern/naming-based). List what you couldn't determine.

Deliver in this order:
1. One-line overlap audit (the exclusions above, filled in).
2. A–D answers, with VERIFIED/INFERRED labels.
3. 5–10 residual tests in plain English, each with a time estimate
   summing to my budget. Rank by signal-per-minute, not completeness.
   For each test, state the specific failure mode it would catch.
4. Sufficiency verdict: is this budget enough for the risk level? If
   not, name the single scenario most likely to cause a prod incident
   and how much time it would add.
5. One "action required on PR" item if testing can't compensate for a
   code-level issue (deleted guard, missing voter, unbackfilled
   migration, unresolved reviewer comment).

name: Coverage Audit
desc: Audit mode on a codebase area (not a diff)
prompt: /goat-qa audit test coverage in a codebase area - ask me which area (default: most-changed files)

name: Regression Guard
desc: Invariant for a recent bug fix
prompt: /goat-qa regression guard for my recent bug fix - ask me for the bug details if not in context

name: User Flow Diagram
desc: Mermaid flow diagram for QA handoff
prompt: /goat-qa produce a Mermaid user-flow diagram - ask me which feature

name: Security Assessment
desc: Full threat assessment with CONFIRMED/PROBABLE/THEORETICAL findings
prompt: /goat-security full assessment of this project

name: Compliance Gap Check
desc: Compliance-mode security assessment with clause citations
prompt: /goat-security compliance mode - ask me which framework (SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, etc.), then present gaps as non-compliant / partially compliant / not assessed with clause citations

name: Dependency Scan
desc: CVE and supply-chain check on dependencies
prompt: /goat-security scan dependencies for known CVEs and outdated packages

name: Critique Security Findings
desc: Multi-lens critique of a security assessment
prompt: /goat-critique this security assessment
